name: d3rlpy (Offline Reinforcement Learning Library)

agent_readiness.notes: Offline (batch) reinforcement learning library. import d3rlpy. dataset = d3rlpy.dataset.MDPDataset(observations, actions, rewards, terminals). cql = d3rlpy.algos.CQLConfig().create(device='cuda:0'). cql.fit(dataset, n_steps=1000000). Algorithms: CQL, IQL, TD3+BC, BCQ, BEAR, AWAC, SAC. Offline RL: learn from static datasets without environment interaction. Scikit-learn style API. Works with D4RL benchmark datasets. Useful for robotics and healthcare RL from logged data.

gotchas: Offline RL is inherently harder to evaluate than online RL
Algorithm selection (CQL vs IQL) requires domain knowledge
D4RL dataset dependency for benchmarks (separate install)
Niche use case — most RL beginners want online RL
best_when: Learning from logged behavior data without environment interaction
Healthcare/finance RL where live exploration is unsafe
Benchmarking offline RL algorithms on D4RL datasets
summary: d3rlpy is an offline (batch) reinforcement learning library with scikit-learn style API. CQLConfig().create().fit(dataset) — train from static logged data without environment interaction. Algorithms: CQL, IQL, TD3+BC, BCQ, BEAR, AWAC. GPU training via PyTorch. D4RL benchmark support. Best for robotics/healthcare/finance RL from demonstration datasets. Use SB3 or CleanRL for online RL.
